steps.title: Identify the hypothesis
steps.content: The researchers hypothesized that interactions showing higher cognitive engagement with ad content would lead to higher ad recall. Commenting and sharing are described as more cognitively demanding than clicking links or using a like button.

steps.title: Reject irrelevant findings
steps.content: Choice A only says which interaction is most common, not whether it improves recall. Choice B concerns purchases, not ad recall. Choice D shows users remembered details after sharing, but its later forgetting detail makes it less direct and does not compare high and lower engagement.

steps.title: Use the direct comparison
steps.content: The remaining finding directly compares a high-engagement action with a lower-engagement action for the same ad and shows higher recall after commenting. The answer is C.
